unit_14 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_14

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 403 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Discovering that cooler stars, which constitute three-quarters of our galaxy, can also be surrounded by habitable zones warm enough to harbor liquid water and, potentially, microbes and bacteria, has (i) the (ii) the search for extraterrestrial life.Discovering that cooler stars, which constitute three-quarters of our galaxy, can also be surrounded by habitable zones warm enough to harbor liquid water and, potentially, microbes and bacteria, has (i) the (ii) the search for extraterrestrial life.
答案：B, D　expanded; target territory in
habitableharbor
habitable: standard unit headword | harbor: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting
exactexacting
exact: standard unit headword | exacting: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
fosterethics
foster: standard unit headword | ethics: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
One baffling aspect of the novel is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most elementary ______: readers must accept not an occasional coincidence, but a continuous stream of them.One baffling aspect of the novel is its capacity to generate emotional power from a plot that lacks the most elementary ______: readers must accept not an occasional coincidence, but a continuous stream of them.
答案：E　credibility
plotsynergy
plot: standard unit headword | synergy: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE

As a result, the animals’ abundance is often (ii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　favorable; lower at the periphery
erratichabitat
erratic: standard unit headword | habitat: standard unit headword

答案：B　insular

答案：A, F　adhere to; abandons

However, they simultaneously have (i)______ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii)______ such fundamental skills truly are.

Blank 2
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted

Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual processes. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification
persist

#15 SE
The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.The difficulty of reforming electoral politics is not lack of the right tools but the need to put them into the hands of impartial agents: the goal should be to build capacity while ______ partisanship.

答案：A, B　manifest; reveal

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

Blank 3

Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.
答案：E　inevitable

答案：D　treasure hunting

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude
depict

答案：D, F　transient; unstable
exceptionaltransient

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce
ensue

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　boom; impose a specious coherence on; complexity

Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to _______ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to _______ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.
答案：B, D　expel; oust

答案：D　insurmountable
insignificant

Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　uncomfortable with; nefarious; an invasion of

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　precipitate; forbore

#64 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

But village cinemas quickly became (ii)______ even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.Until the advent of film, commercial entertainment in England occurred only where concentrated urban populations provided audiences large enough to make it remunerative; theaters and music halls were (i)______ in rural villages. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound

Establishing the exact boundaries of a piece of property can be __________ process; some of the bitterest battles in contemporary society are waged between neighbors fighting over small slivers of land.Establishing the exact boundaries of a piece of property can be __________ process; some of the bitterest battles in contemporary society are waged between neighbors fighting over small slivers of land.
答案：D　a fraught
exact
exact: standard unit headword
#105 SE
An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so ______.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematic formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so ______.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar
paradox
paradox: standard unit headword

The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted

#107 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to

plot: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
One of the reasons why so many rodent species live in rocky habitat is because it is __________; it contains plenty of crevices and cavities in which animals can nest, seek shelter, and find shade.One of the reasons why so many rodent species live in rocky habitat is because it is __________; it contains plenty of crevices and cavities in which animals can nest, seek shelter, and find shade.
答案：D　topographically complex
habitat
habitat: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of ______ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of ______ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting

#110 TC TRIPLE
In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of
omission
omission: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Early animals were primarily decomposers, feeding off detritus left by plants. However, as the Carboniferous period progressed, insects began to eat the plants themselves, a development that would have (i) __________ natural selection of plants with (ii) __________ plant tissues and perhaps early chemical defense mechanisms.Early animals were primarily decomposers, feeding off detritus left by plants. However, as the Carboniferous period progressed, insects began to eat the plants themselves, a development that would have (i) __________ natural selection of plants with (ii) __________ plant tissues and perhaps early chemical defense mechanisms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　promoted; tougher

#114 TC TRIPLE
One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt

#115 TC DOUBLE

This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.Audiences for daytime television soap operas have declined by 83 percent since 1991. This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) __________ that appeal to soap fans. And courtroom dramas have the added (ii) __________ that the domestic arguments they depict are for real.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　wrangling; frisson

Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.
答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed

答案：E　segregation

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

#119 TC DOUBLE
Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. But as temperatures rise the permafrost recedes, (i)______ the (ii)______ of forest.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　facilitating; incursion

答案：C　pedestrian

There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. 答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious

However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii) those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii) in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i) effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii) those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii) in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
habitat
habitat: standard unit headword

Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii) in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii) on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i) incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii) in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Thus, relative to large craters, small craters are much less (iii) on bodies with dense atmosphere, such as Earth, Venus, and Titan, than they are on Mercury and Moon.

It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. The former are simply wrong, the latter can be seen as incomplete, overly simplistic, or tied to only a few limited contexts.In science education, it is important to differentiate between inaccurate ideas that are conceptually (i) and understandings that are inaccurate, and yet can (ii) learning of more sophisticated understanding. #293 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
flee
flee: standard unit headword

#343 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious

appreciate: standard unit headword
#392 SE
Far from ______ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.Far from ______ innovations, as the patent system was designed to do, the patenting of concepts such as gene sequences give individuals and corporations a legal choke to hold over ideas that should be useful to all.
答案：A, E　spurring; fostering
fostercatalog
foster: standard unit headword | catalog: standard unit headword
#393 SE
The new edition of Middleton's collected works is __________ achievement that only someone with Taylor's infectious energy and obsessive determination could have brought off.The new edition of Middleton's collected works is __________ achievement that only someone with Taylor's infectious energy and obsessive determination could have brought off.
答案：D, E　a prodigious; an immense
peculiar
peculiar: standard unit headword
#394 SE
Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.Fedigan argues that, in actuality, ethologists who claim to __________ anthropomorphism often end up simply substituting one set of anthropomorphic terms for another.
答案：A, F　eschew; avoid
appreciate
appreciate: standard unit headword
#395 SE
Suburbanization arguably ushered in numerous changes in the way many people in the United States lived: the suburbs __________ a society based on unprecedented consumption and intense privacy.Suburbanization arguably ushered in numerous changes in the way many people in the United States lived: the suburbs __________ a society based on unprecedented consumption and intense privacy.
答案：C, D　inaugurated; engendered

答案：A, E　spurring; fostering

#397 SE
Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had ______ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had ______ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

#398 SE
Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i)__—_—sccertain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) such fundamental skills truly are. [(#UBARMS: Sk /AGRE)Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i)__—_—sccertain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) such fundamental skills truly are. [(#UBARMS: Sk /AGRE)
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive

#399 SE
Liam Clancy described the young Bob Dylan as a sponge, eagerly absorbing the possibilities life and culture might provide, and Dylan presents himself so in his memoir _____in his intellectual and musical curiosity. [fs ARS: 3kIMGRE)Liam Clancy described the young Bob Dylan as a sponge, eagerly absorbing the possibilities life and culture might provide, and Dylan presents himself so in his memoir _____in his intellectual and musical curiosity. [fs ARS: 3kIMGRE)
答案：A, E　insatiable; voracious

#400 SE
Even though Our Lady of the Forest achieves clinical evenhandedness in depicting the most depressing members of a small logging town, the novel lacks a single likable character and too often runs aground on ___________ writing.Even though Our Lady of the Forest achieves clinical evenhandedness in depicting the most depressing members of a small logging town, the novel lacks a single likable character and too often runs aground on ___________ writing.
答案：A, D　stilted; wooden

#401 SE
While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.
答案：A, C　parochial; narrow

#402 SE
An affinity for (i) is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii) can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.An affinity for (i) is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii) can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.
答案：A, F　paradox; unresolvable ambivalences

paradox: standard unit headword | affinity: standard unit headword
#403 SE
With their __________ energy production, massive stars create harsh environments for planetary genesis and may inhibit planet formation altogether: their intense radiation can readily destroy planetary birth sites before new worlds can be constructed.With their __________ energy production, massive stars create harsh environments for planetary genesis and may inhibit planet formation altogether: their intense radiation can readily destroy planetary birth sites before new worlds can be constructed.
答案：B, D　profligate; extravagant
